title: Crashed on Silverblue (via toolbox) with different shell on host than in pod
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman crashes.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I am not entirely sure, but if you have:
* zsh on the host 
* in the pod, I have also had ` zsh`  installed (but that does not actually matter, I had the same crash before when there was no zsh in the pod)
* `toolbox enter`

The image is all defaults, i.e. toolbox Fedora 30 image.

For more complete STRs/the whole backstory, please refer to https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/t/toolbox-and-zsh/2035?u=rugk

**Describe the results you received:**

```sh
$ toolbox enter
/usr/bin/toolbox: Zeile 1126: 31015 Aborted                 (Speicherabzug geschrieben) $prefix_sudo podman container exists "$toolbox_container_old_v1" 2>&3
/usr/bin/toolbox: Zeile 1126: 31032 Aborted                 (Speicherabzug geschrieben) $prefix_sudo podman container exists "$toolbox_container_old_v2" 2>&3
toolbox: container fedora-toolbox-30 not found
Entering container fedora-toolbox-30 instead.
Use the 'create' command to create a different toolbox.
Try 'toolbox --help' for more information.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
no crash

If you re-enter the toolbox later, it does not happen again. (only after a restart)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**


```sh
$ podman version                                         
Version:            1.4.5-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.6
  podman version: 1.4.5-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-1.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.2.0, commit: 41010e63c287618b1dc34ee11d10d268e2feeefe'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  MemFree: 223256576
  MemTotal: 3965403136
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-93.dev.gitb9b6cc6.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc8+dev
      commit: e3b4c1108f7d1bf0d09ab612ea09927d9b59b4e3
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 3567251456
  SwapTotal: 4102025216
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: ATravel
  kernel: 5.1.16-300.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 1h 33m 48.11s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/rugk/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /var/home/rugk/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
  VolumePath: /var/home/rugk/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora Silverblue 30
30.20190707.0 (2019-07-07T00:38:42Z)
BaseCommit: 751d21e610448544298604e5cd984179e62439cb253939cbb4d371d0661e1e74

---


I have to correct myself, when executing this one command, it actually crashes **three times**:
```sh
$ coredumpctl list -r  
TIME                            PID   UID   GID SIG COREFILE  EXE
Mon 2019-07-08 20:56:19 CEST  31032  1000  1000   6 present   /usr/bin/podman
Mon 2019-07-08 20:56:17 CEST  31015  1000  1000   6 present   /usr/bin/podman
Mon 2019-07-08 20:56:15 CEST  30989  1000  1000   6 present   /usr/bin/podman
[…]

comments.body:
Eek.
@debarshiray Are you folks doing any mounts related to entrypoint shell in toolbox?

The entry-point specified in `podman create ...` is also the `toolbox(1)` command with a set of specific flags. This is done this way to let the container self-configure itself at run-time. It works by having the toolbox script bind mounted into the container.

Is that what you meant by *mounts related to entrypoint shell* ?

The crash is caused by initial calls to `podman container exists fedora-toolbox-30`
(as seen when running `toolbox -v enter`).

```
$ podman container exists fedora-toolbox-30

I can reproduce the `podman container exists` crash after a fresh reboot, before running any other podman command.
ok there were recent changes there ... if you reboot and run a podman command like "create" and then the same exists command, does it fail?

@baude I see a `GetRuntimeNoStore()` in the call stack and then podman crashes when it tries to access the storage in `GetRunDir()`.  Looks like we need to use `GetRuntime()` if `refresh` wasn't already called

> if you reboot and run a podman command like "create" and then the same exists command, does it fail?

(No it doesn't)
Alright. I think we need to detect a refresh and conditionally ignore NoStore if we need one.
